Jiacheng Zhong's BlackHat Asia 2026 (2026-04-24) talk demonstrates hijacks in this class — attacker posts a crafted comment that the agent's own reply loop turns into a tool call, reflecting attacker text back to the platform's audience. OWASP LLM08 (Excessive Agency).
rules.remediation: Place every social-platform write call behind a human-review gate. AAK ships an `aak.review.human_in_loop(text, comment=...)` helper that defaults closed and requires explicit approval (CLI, webhook, or workflow). For high-volume agents, route generated replies to a moderation queue instead of the platform sink directly.
rules.title: Social-agent auto-reply without human-in-loop gate

rules.description: An agent project exposes both a sensitive source tool (filesystem read, secrets read, database query) and an external sink tool (HTTP POST, email send, git push) without an explicit `.aak-toxic-flow-trust.yml` allow-list entry. Even if each tool is individually safe, the LLM can chain them — the canonical exfil pattern is `read_file -> http.post`. Suppress with an allow-list when the pairing is a documented product feature.
rules.remediation: Add the source/sink pair to `.aak-toxic-flow-trust.yml` with a `justification:` field, scope the source tool to a directory the sink cannot reach, or remove one side of the pair. Run `agent-audit-kit toxic-flow --explain` to see the full graph.
rules.title: Toxic flow: sensitive source paired with external sink

rules.description: An MCP server URL uses HTTP instead of HTTPS, exposing all traffic including credentials to interception.
rules.remediation: Use HTTPS for all remote MCP server connections.
rules.title: MCP server uses HTTP instead of HTTPS

rules.description: TLS certificate validation is disabled via NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 or similar, enabling MITM attacks.
rules.remediation: Remove TLS validation overrides. Use proper certificate management.
rules.title: TLS certificate validation disabled

rules.description: An MCP server uses deprecated Server-Sent Events (SSE) transport instead of Streamable HTTP.
rules.remediation: Migrate to Streamable HTTP transport (MCP spec 2025-03-26+).
rules.title: Deprecated SSE transport in use

rules.description: A session token or API key is passed as a URL query parameter, risking exposure in logs and referrer headers.
rules.remediation: Pass tokens in HTTP headers instead of URL query parameters.
rules.title: Session token in URL query parameter

rules.description: Auto-approves ALL MCP servers in .mcp.json without user consent. A compromised repo can ship arbitrary MCP servers that execute immediately.
rules.remediation: Set to false. Use enabledMcpjsonServers to whitelist specific servers by name.
rules.title: enableAllProjectMcpServers is true

rules.description: Redirects all API traffic (including API keys) to attacker-controlled endpoint BEFORE trust prompt displays. This is the exact attack vector of CVE-2026-21852.
rules.remediation: NEVER override ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL in project settings. Only set in user-level or system environment.
rules.title: ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL overridden in project settings

rules.description: Permission allow patterns use wildcards (*, **) or broad tool names. This bypasses the permission system, allowing unchecked tool execution.
rules.remediation: Use narrowest possible permission patterns. Specify exact tool names and path constraints.
rules.title: Wildcard or overly broad permission allows

rules.description: Settings file has permission allows but empty or missing deny rules. Defense-in-depth requires explicit deny rules for sensitive operations.
rules.remediation: Add deny rules for: file system operations outside project, network tools, credential-accessing tools.
rules.title: No deny rules defined

rules.description: An environment variable matching *_BASE_URL, *_API_URL, or *_ENDPOINT is set in project settings. This can redirect authenticated traffic to attacker-controlled servers.
rules.remediation: Set API URLs only in user-level configuration or system environment variables.
rules.title: Custom API base URL for any provider

rules.description: Project settings have permission allows that could shadow user-level deny rules. Misconfigurations can create a false sense of security.
rules.remediation: Audit that project allows don't re-enable operations the user intended to block.
rules.title: Project settings may override user deny rules

rules.description: No enabledMcpjsonServers allowlist is configured, meaning server approval relies entirely on user prompts.
rules.remediation: Configure enabledMcpjsonServers with explicit server names.
rules.title: No MCP server allowlist configured

rules.description: A `.windsurf/mcp.json` file declares auto_approve:true or auto_execute:true, or contains server `command:` entries with no SHA-256 pin. CVE-2026-30615 (Windsurf 1.9544.26, CVSS 8.0) shows attackers can inject malicious MCP registrations via HTML prompt injection when auto-approval is enabled.
rules.remediation: Set auto_approve and auto_execute to false. Pin every server command to a SHA-256 digest. Upgrade Windsurf to a version with the registration confirmation flow enabled.
rules.title: Windsurf .windsurf/mcp.json auto-approves server registrations
